units.title: Classroom Language
units.topic: Words and phrases used in the classroom every day

words.description: A large flat surface on the wall where a teacher writes with chalk or a marker
words.example: The teacher wrote the new words on the board.

words.description: A small book with blank or lined pages used for writing notes
words.example: Write the vocabulary in your notebook.

words.description: A book used for studying a particular subject at school or college
words.example: Open your textbook to page 12.

words.description: A book or digital tool that explains the meaning of words in a language
words.example: Use a dictionary to find the meaning of new words.

words.description: A writing tool that uses ink to write on paper
words.example: Can I borrow your pen? I need to sign something.

words.description: A thin wooden stick with a dark centre used for writing or drawing that can be erased
words.example: Draw the diagram with a pencil so you can erase mistakes.

words.description: School work that a student is given to do at home, not in class
words.example: The teacher gave us homework to do for tomorrow.

words.description: A set of questions or tasks done to practise a skill
words.example: Do exercise 3 on page 25 for homework.

words.description: To say or do something again
words.example: Please repeat that — I didn't hear you.

words.description: To know the meaning of something; to comprehend
words.example: Do you understand the grammar rule?

words.description: Right or accurate; or to fix a mistake
words.example: That answer is correct! / The teacher will correct your essays.

words.description: Something that is wrong or done incorrectly
words.example: Everyone makes mistakes when learning a language.

units.title: Countries and Nationalities
units.topic: Names of countries, languages spoken, and how to describe where people come from

words.description: A nation with its own government and borders, e.g. France, Japan, Brazil
words.example: Which country are you from?

words.description: The status of belonging to a particular country
words.example: My nationality is Uzbek — I am from Uzbekistan.

words.description: A system of words and grammar used by people of a country or community to communicate
words.example: The official language of Brazil is Portuguese.

words.description: The most important city in a country where the government is based
words.example: London is the capital of England.

words.description: One of the seven large land masses on Earth: Africa, Asia, Europe, North America, South America, Australia, Antarctica
words.example: Uzbekistan is in the continent of Asia.

words.description: In or to a foreign country, outside your own country
words.example: She went abroad to study English in the UK.

words.description: An official document that proves your identity and nationality when travelling to other countries
words.example: You need a passport to travel abroad.

words.description: Belonging to or coming from a country that is not your own
words.example: She speaks two foreign languages: English and French.

words.description: The total number of people who live in a particular place
words.example: The population of Uzbekistan is about 36 million.

words.description: A person who legally belongs to a country and has rights there
words.example: She became a citizen of the United States after living there for years.

words.description: The line that separates one country or region from another
words.example: Uzbekistan shares a border with Kazakhstan.

words.description: Relating to the place where you were born or grew up
words.example: Uzbek is my native language.

units.title: The Family
units.topic: Words for family members and relationships between people

words.description: Your mother and father together
words.example: My parents are both teachers.

words.description: A brother or sister
words.example: I have two siblings — one brother and one sister.

words.description: The parents of your mother or father
words.example: I visit my grandparents every weekend.

words.description: The brother of your mother or father, or the husband of your aunt
words.example: My uncle lives in another city.

words.description: The sister of your mother or father, or the wife of your uncle
words.example: My aunt makes the best food.

words.description: The child of your aunt or uncle
words.example: I have five cousins on my mother's side.

words.description: The son of your brother or sister
words.example: My nephew is four years old.

words.description: The daughter of your brother or sister
words.example: My niece started school this year.

words.description: A man who is married; the male partner in a marriage
words.example: Her husband works as an engineer.

words.description: A woman who is married; the female partner in a marriage
words.example: His wife is a doctor.

words.description: A child who has no brothers or sisters
words.example: He is an only child, so he grew up without siblings.

words.description: A member of your family; someone related to you by blood or marriage
words.example: We have relatives living in many different cities.

units.title: Jobs and Occupations
units.topic: Common words for different kinds of work and the places where people work

words.description: A person who is trained in medicine and treats people who are sick or injured
words.example: The doctor told me to rest and drink plenty of water.

words.description: A person trained to care for sick or injured people, often working in hospitals
words.example: The nurse checked my blood pressure every morning.

words.description: A person whose job is to educate students in a school or other setting
words.example: My English teacher is very patient and helpful.

words.description: A person who designs and builds machines, structures, or systems
words.example: She works as a software engineer at a tech company.

words.description: A person who is trained in law and helps people with legal problems
words.example: We hired a lawyer to help us with the contract.

words.description: A professional cook who works in a restaurant or hotel kitchen
words.example: The chef at this restaurant makes amazing food.

words.description: A person who is trained to fly an aircraft
words.example: The pilot landed the plane safely in bad weather.

words.description: A person who writes or reports news for newspapers, TV, radio, or online
words.example: The journalist wrote an article about the new law.

words.description: A person who designs buildings and oversees their construction
words.example: The architect designed a beautiful new library.

words.description: A person who manages financial records and taxes for businesses or individuals
words.example: Our accountant handles all the company's finances.

words.description: A fixed amount of money paid to an employee regularly, usually monthly
words.example: She earns a good salary at her new job.

words.description: Not having a job; without paid work
words.example: He was unemployed for six months before finding a new job.

units.title: The Body
units.topic: Names of the main parts of the human body, both external and related actions

words.description: The top part of the body containing the brain, eyes, nose, mouth, and ears
words.example: She shook her head to say no.

words.description: The joint connecting the arm to the upper body; the area between the neck and the arm
words.example: He carried the bag over his shoulder.

words.description: The joint in the middle of the arm where it bends
words.example: I bumped my elbow on the table.

words.description: The joint that connects the hand to the lower arm
words.example: She wears a watch on her wrist.

words.description: The short thick finger at the side of the hand, separate from the other four fingers
words.example: He gave a thumbs-up to show everything was OK.

words.description: The joint in the middle of the leg that allows it to bend
words.example: She hurt her knee while playing football.

words.description: The joint that connects the foot to the lower leg
words.example: He twisted his ankle when he fell.

words.description: The part of the face below the mouth and above the neck
words.example: She rested her chin on her hand while thinking.

words.description: The flat part of the face above the eyebrows and below the hairline
words.example: She had a headache and rubbed her forehead.

words.description: Either side of the face below the eyes
words.example: The baby had round, pink cheeks.

words.description: The two soft edges around the opening of the mouth
words.example: Drink water if your lips feel dry.

words.description: The front of the body between the neck and the stomach; contains the heart and lungs
words.example: The doctor listened to his chest with a stethoscope.

units.title: Clothes
units.topic: Types of clothing, accessories, and words for describing what people wear

words.description: A short coat with sleeves, worn over other clothes
words.example: Take a jacket — it will be cold tonight.

words.description: A piece of clothing that covers the lower body with separate legs; called 'pants' in American English
words.example: He wore black trousers to the interview.

words.description: A piece of clothing for the upper body with a collar and buttons, usually worn by men
words.example: He always wears a white shirt to work.

words.description: A loose shirt typically worn by women
words.example: She wore a silk blouse with her skirt.

words.description: A piece of clothing worn by women and girls that hangs from the waist down
words.example: She wore a long skirt to the party.

words.description: A one-piece garment for women or girls that covers the body from the shoulders to below the waist
words.example: She bought a beautiful red dress for the wedding.

words.description: A set of matching jacket and trousers (or skirt) worn for formal occasions
words.example: He wore a dark blue suit to the job interview.

words.description: Comfortable sports shoes, called 'sneakers' in American English
words.example: I wear trainers when I go to the gym.

words.description: Strong shoes that cover the foot and ankle, sometimes higher up the leg
words.example: She wears boots in winter to keep her feet warm.

words.description: A long piece of cloth worn around the neck or head for warmth or style
words.example: He wrapped a scarf around his neck on the cold day.

words.description: Coverings for the hands with separate parts for each finger, worn for warmth or protection
words.example: Don't forget your gloves — it's freezing outside.

words.description: A strip of leather or cloth worn around the waist to hold up trousers or as decoration
words.example: His trousers were too big, so he wore a belt.

units.title: Food and Drink
units.topic: Common food items, drinks, and vocabulary for eating and cooking

words.description: A basic food made from flour and water and baked in an oven; a staple in many countries
words.example: She bought fresh bread from the bakery.

words.description: A soft yellow fat made from cream, spread on bread or used in cooking
words.example: He spread butter on his toast.

words.description: A solid food made from milk, available in many different types and flavours
words.example: She put cheese and tomato in her sandwich.

words.description: Plants or parts of plants eaten as food, such as carrots, onions, and tomatoes
words.example: Try to eat more vegetables every day.

words.description: The sweet, fleshy product of a plant, eaten as food; e.g. apples, bananas, oranges
words.example: She had fruit and yoghurt for breakfast.

words.description: The flesh of animals eaten as food, such as beef, chicken, or lamb
words.example: He doesn't eat meat — he's a vegetarian.

words.description: Small white or brown grains from a plant, cooked and eaten as a staple food in many countries
words.example: In Uzbekistan, rice is used to make plov.

words.description: A liquid food made by cooking meat or vegetables in water or stock
words.example: She made tomato soup for lunch.

words.description: A drink made from the liquid extracted from fruit or vegetables
words.example: I drink orange juice every morning.

words.description: A small amount of food eaten between main meals
words.example: I had an apple as a snack between classes.

words.description: A set of instructions for preparing a particular food dish, listing ingredients and steps
words.example: Can you share the recipe for this cake?

words.description: Having a very pleasant taste or smell; extremely good to eat
words.example: This samsa is absolutely delicious!

units.title: Colors
units.topic: Basic and advanced color words, and how to use them to describe things

words.description: The color of blood or fire; a warm, strong primary color
words.example: She wore a red dress to the party.

words.description: The color of the sky on a clear day or the ocean; a cool primary color
words.example: He has bright blue eyes.

words.description: The color of grass and leaves; associated with nature
words.example: The park is full of green trees.

words.description: A bright warm color like the sun or ripe bananas
words.example: Sunflowers are bright yellow.

words.description: A warm color between red and yellow, like the fruit of the same name
words.example: The autumn leaves turned orange.

words.description: A color made by mixing red and blue; often associated with royalty
words.example: She painted the wall purple.

words.description: A dark warm color like chocolate, wood, or earth
words.example: He has brown hair and brown eyes.

words.description: A neutral color between black and white, like clouds on an overcast day
words.example: It was a grey, cloudy morning.

words.description: The lightest color, the color of snow or milk; the opposite of black
words.example: The walls in the hospital are white.

words.description: The darkest color, absorbing all light; the opposite of white
words.example: He always wears black clothes.

words.description: Used before a color to mean a pale, not strong version of that color
words.example: She wore a light blue blouse.

words.description: Used before a color to mean a deep, strong version of that color
words.example: He was wearing dark green trousers.

units.title: Days, Months and Time
units.topic: Days of the week, months of the year, and expressions for talking about time

words.description: Any day of the week from Monday to Friday, when people usually work or study
words.example: The library is open every weekday from 9am to 6pm.

words.description: Saturday and Sunday, when most people do not work
words.example: What did you do at the weekend?

words.description: The day before today
words.example: I was tired yesterday, so I went to bed early.

words.description: The day after today
words.example: We have an English test tomorrow.

words.description: A period of two weeks (14 days); commonly used in British English
words.example: I'll see you in a fortnight.

words.description: A period of 100 years
words.example: We are living in the 21st century.

words.description: A period of ten years
words.example: A lot has changed in the last decade.

words.description: 12 o'clock in the middle of the day; midday
words.example: We had lunch at noon.

words.description: 12 o'clock at night; the middle of the night
words.example: The party went on until midnight.

words.description: The time of day when light first appears; the beginning of the morning
words.example: The farmer woke up at dawn to start work.

words.uzbek: choraklik, har uch oyda bir marta
words.description: Happening four times a year, once every three months
words.example: The company publishes a quarterly report.

words.description: Happening once every year
words.example: The school holds an annual sports day in June.

units.title: The Home
units.topic: Rooms of a house, furniture, and objects found in the home

words.description: The main room in a home used for relaxing, watching TV, and spending time with family
words.example: We usually watch TV in the living room in the evening.

words.description: A room in a house used for sleeping
words.example: Their house has three bedrooms.

words.description: The room in a house where food is prepared and cooked
words.example: She was cooking in the kitchen when I arrived.

words.description: A room with a bath or shower, sink, and usually a toilet
words.example: Can I use your bathroom?

words.description: The upper interior surface of a room; the part above your head when inside a building
words.example: The ceiling in the old house was very high.

words.description: The lower surface of a room that you walk on
words.example: The floor is wet — be careful!

words.description: A series of steps inside a building that allow you to move between floors
words.example: Take the stairs — the lift is broken.

words.description: Large moveable objects in a room such as tables, chairs, beds, and wardrobes
words.example: They bought new furniture for their apartment.

words.description: Pieces of fabric hung over windows to block light or provide privacy
words.example: She opened the curtains to let the sunlight in.

words.description: A large piece of furniture with doors used for storing clothes
words.example: He hung his suits in the wardrobe.

words.description: A flat board fixed to a wall or inside a cupboard, used for storing things
words.example: The books are on the shelf above the desk.

words.description: A platform with a railing outside an upper floor of a building
words.example: We had breakfast on the balcony every morning.
